misleading_signal: the traversal and tile comment make the access pattern look cache-friendly, while the kernel spends many arithmetic ops per fetched element
the kernel performs a clean streaming load/store pattern, so it looks like a bandwidth-limited map over contiguous memory
the branch-local barrier looks like the obvious defect, even though the real cost comes from pointer-chasing through next_slot
the 32x32 shared-memory staging looks like deliberate textbook tiling, but the staged buffers quietly consume enough SM resources to crush occupancy
the kernel reads contiguous windows from two arrays and looks like a straightforward streaming filter, but the real issue is excessive per-thread state
